title: About | Mark Hendrick
text_excerpt: --> About | Mark Hendrick top of page Sir Mark Hendrick Labour and Co-operative MP for Preston Home Contact About News Newsletters Parliament Written Questions Media Gallery More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. mark.hendrick.mp@parliament.uk 01772 883575 About A former electronics engineer and college lecturer, Sir Mark began his political career as a Salford City Councillor in 1987, before being elected as a Member of the European Parliament for Central Lancashire in 1994. ​ Sir Mark became MP for Preston at the November 2000 by-election and has been re-elected at every General Election since. He has been on several influential parliamentary committees including the Foreign Affairs Select Committee, International Development Select Committee, European Scrutiny Select Committee and the International Trade Select Committee. He is also serves on Mr Speaker's Panel of Chairs and is currently the Chair of the APPG for Germany, Japan and Vietnam. ​ Sir Mark was awarded a Knighthood for services to Parliamentary and Political Service in the Queen’s New Year’s Honours List 2018.
